result.summary: TRIGGERcmd exposes an MCP server at `https://triggercmd.com/mcp` protected by OAuth 2.0 against `https://triggercmd.com`; the corpus does not document any other public developer surface.

triggercmd_oauth2.label: TRIGGERcmd OAuth 2.0 client/app and user authorization
triggercmd_oauth2.setup: Register an OAuth client at [TRIGGERcmd registration](https://triggercmd.com/register), then authorize a user against [TRIGGERcmd](https://triggercmd.com). The detected OAuth endpoints are `https://triggercmd.com/oauth/authorize` for authorization and `https://triggercmd.com/oauth/token` for token exchange. Use the resulting bearer access token in the MCP connection.

surfaces.name: TRIGGERcmd MCP Server
surfaces.notes: Detected as an OAuth 2.0 protected MCP endpoint with authorization server `https://triggercmd.com` and dynamic client registration support. The protected resource metadata at `https://triggercmd.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` advertises bearer tokens in headers.
